title: calophyllum parvifolium
intro: Calophyllum parvifolium, also known as the small-leaved Alexandrian laurel, is a plant species native to Australia and New Guinea, characterized by its small, elliptical leaves and fragrant flowers. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial effects, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Calophyllum parvifolium include the treatment of skin conditions, such as wounds, eczema, and acne, as well as its potential to aid in the management of pain and fever. Herbal preparations of Calophyllum parvifolium include infusions, extracts, and essential oils, which can be used topically or internally, depending on the specific health concern being addressed.
